note: Quarter-end repo turn: SOFR fixed +3bp over IORB at Q2 end (99th pct 3.80%), normalized within 2 days; no SRF borrowing. One of the mildest recent turns (vs +25bp Sep-2024, record $50B SRF Oct-2025). NY Fed (Perli, Jul 9): reserves ample, early-July softness seasonal.

Spread flipped negative (-4 pips, CNH stronger): HK quarter-end CNH liquidity squeeze (MOF offshore-bond demand pushed CNH funding costs to 2-month high) on a backdrop of yuan appreciation pressure (+2.3% YTD) with PBOC fixings anchoring onshore.

July-4 holiday-weekend Sunday dip stacked with real disruption: US heat-dome thunderstorms (182 cancellations/693 delays peaking near the 22:00 UTC sample) after a Jul-4 ORD ground stop, plus a nationwide Italy aviation strike incl. 24h ENAV Milan ACC walkout (438 flights) inside the W/C Europe circle. Possible amplification from ADS-B provider flakiness that week.

Guam: Super Typhoon Bavi (Cat 5, 180mph) landfall Rota/Guam Jul 5-6 destroyed much of the power grid — genuine ~5.4-day outage, score ~155k. The wider 16-18-country spike (Jul 8-10) is mostly an IODA monitoring artifact: gtr datasource began emitting country events Jul 1, bgp/merit-nt on Jul 5 (52/89 spike events are merit-nt micro-events in North Macedonia); ping-slash24 stayed at baseline.

Spread -20 pips (CNH stronger), same day as PBOC Governor Pan Gongsheng's Hong Kong offshore-yuan package at the Bond Connect Forum: Southbound quota 500bn->800bn yuan, RMB Business Facility doubled to 500bn, more FX reserves to HK assets — an offshore-specific bullish CNH catalyst.

Up-move (2017 transits, direction=up = MORE ships): Strait of Hormuz reopened — from ~2/day under the June blockade to 22-42/day by early July. A de-escalation signal; the alarming direction for this line is down.

Observation dedup: rows gained an obs_date column; the z baseline now uses only the first occurrence of each observation (lagged sources republish readings, and duplicates shrink MAD / inflate step z). A republished observation scores no new z and raises no flag. Windows also cap at 180 calendar days. Applied forward only; historical z/trembling values were not rewritten (obs_date was backfilled from each row's own source_note). Backtest over the 19-day history: the chokepoint/port/sofr publication-step trembles of 07-02..07-10 would not have fired under these rules.

Direction-aware resonance: the trembling flag still records |z|>3 in either direction, but trembling_count now counts only trembles in a line's declared alarm direction. A guard visibly reasserting itself (e.g. a strait reopening) is recorded as a benign shift, not disorder. Applied forward only.

Weekly-cycle warm-up veto: until a cyclic line has 10 same-weekday samples, a full-window tremble is suppressed when today's level lies within the same-weekday min-max envelope (nonparametric; a genuine crisis value cannot be vetoed). Every suppression is written into source_note.

ADS-B region sanity floor: a provider reporting under ~30 aircraft for a region is treated as a suspected degraded feed and the next provider is tried; two providers agreeing under-floor is accepted as a real reading (corroboration). Per-region counts now recorded in source_note. Also applies to cn_flights.

Series v2: the conflict share is now aggregated over the ENTIRE previous UTC day (~96 15-minute files, ~100k events) instead of sampling one file, and sets obs_date. v1 was noise-dominated (8->23% inside a week); archived at data/archive/gdelt_v1.csv. v2 starts fresh.
New feel line: full-day average news tone from the same GDELT aggregation pass. Contrast only (no guard), never counted.

Series v2: the count is now restricted to IODA's ping-slash24 datasource. The v1 series counted every datasource, and IODA activating new detectors mid-series (gtr 07-01, bgp/merit-nt 07-05) inflated the count — sensor inflation, not disorder. v1 archived at data/archive/net_outages_v1.csv; v2 starts fresh under warm-up.

Start of a soft-funding run: SOFR fell to 7, then 12, then 10 bp BELOW the IORB ceiling (obs Jul 8-10) before firming and softening again to -8 bp by obs Jul 20. This is ample-reserve softness, not stress. The NY Fed's SOMA manager diagnosed it publicly on Jul 9, noting repo had gone as low as 15 bp below IORB in mid-May: a downward shift in the reserve demand curve from seasonally low bill supply, more dealer repo intermediation, lower repo financing demand and a temporary rise in GSE repo investment, plus reserve supply from a falling TGA and ongoing reserve management purchases — with no evidence of a material change in banks' demand for reserves (reserves ~$3.1T, ON RRP ~zero). The alarm direction for this line is UP: stress is the spread compressing toward zero and going positive, as in Sep-Nov 2025. Watch August, when heavy net bill issuance (~$348bn in the Jul-Sep quarter, TGA rebuilding toward ~$1T) may push it back up. The three down-trembles were correctly excluded from the resonance count.

New feel line: VIX daily close via keyless FRED (VIXCLS). Excluded as a tension indicator by the spec (no guard) — admitted as a CONTRAST line, whose job is measuring priced fear. Seeded with 180 days of archive scored through the standard pipeline, so its baseline needed no warm-up; seed rows are dated by observation date and noted as archive imports.

166 mHz daily maximum tripped the threshold with no Nordic or Baltic power system incident found — no generator trip, HVDC fault or nuclear outage on or near the date (Olkiluoto's 2026 annual outages ran Apr 6-16 and Apr 19-Jun 13, both finished; its only 2026 trip was Jan 2). The threshold is simply too tight: Fingrid's normal band is +/-100 mHz and the Nordic frequency leaves it roughly 27 minutes every day, while Fingrid's own bar for a large deviation is >300 mHz — 11 such events in all of 2024. Mid-July is also the low-inertia, low-load season, structurally the worst for frequency quality. See the paired method row: a daily MAXIMUM is an extreme-value statistic and is mis-specified under a symmetric MAD z.

186 mHz daily maximum, same cause as 2026-07-12: no incident found, and 186 mHz sits far below Fingrid's own 300 mHz large-deviation bar. Two false trembles in 31 days (a 9.5% rate) is the evidence that this line's statistic, not the world, is the problem.

Sat Jul 18 evening: US East fell to 331 aircraft (14-day median ~729) while Europe, US West and Japan stayed normal. SPC had an enhanced risk of severe thunderstorms across the Northeast/Mid-Atlantic/Lower Great Lakes with the strongest storms afternoon into early evening — the 22:54 UTC sample sits inside the peak. Over 1,000 US cancellations that Saturday; a separate nationwide United systems failure (~06:00-07:17 CT) helped drive 2,437 cancellations across Jul 18-19. Caveat: Saturday-evening traffic is structurally below the weekday baseline, so part of this drop is day-of-week and part is storm. Did not trip (z=-2.802).

Global news tone fell to -2.323, the first tremble on the felt side of the instrument. Checked against the weekend artifact and it survives: this line has a strong uncorrected weekly cycle (weekend ~61,876 events and tone -2.100 versus weekday ~108,245 and -1.897), but the Monday Jul 20 observation is -2.114 against a weekday-only baseline mean of -1.877, i.e. -4.31 sd among weekdays alone. The cause was real. The Jun 17 US-Iran ceasefire had collapsed; US forces struck 80+ targets inside Iran over Jul 12-13 and were on a tenth consecutive night of strikes by Jul 21; the IRGC closed the Strait of Hormuz; on Jul 20 the Houthis declared a full naval blockade of Saudi Arabia and the Pentagon identified two more US soldiers killed in Jordan (at least 17 killed in total); Iran widened strikes to Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan; three tankers burned in Hormuz; Brent settled +2% at $91.01.

MISS RECORDED. Between Jul 7 and Jul 22 the tier-1 resonance read 0 of 4 every single day. During that window the US-Iran ceasefire collapsed, the Strait of Hormuz was closed, the Houthis declared a naval blockade of Saudi Arabia, at least 17 US service members were killed and Brent reached $91. The instrument was not wrong about its own lines — credit spreads (2.69), the yuan spread and GPS interference genuinely did not strain — but it had no sensor pointed at the Gulf. flights watches only W/C Europe, US East, US West and E Asia/Japan; gnss_interference sums every h3 cell on earth into a single global ratio and so has no regional sensitivity at all; the one line watching Hormuz is tier-2, lags about eight days, and could not score. A reading of 0/4 therefore meant 'we were not looking there', while the dashboard presented it as 'the instruments are calm'. Logged before any fix, so the failure is part of the permanent record rather than an embarrassment edited out of it.

The Strait of Hormuz closure is visible in this line's RAW values — global chokepoint transits fell from 2,137 (obs Jul 12) to 1,828 (obs Jul 19), about -14% — but the line emitted NO z-score and raised NO flag. IMF PortWatch updates weekly, and after the observation-dedup rule this source has fewer than the 10 unique observations needed to judge honestly; it cannot score until roughly October 2026. The raw move is recorded here precisely because the instrument could not score it. This is the clearest case yet that a line which cannot score must be DISCLOSED as structurally blind rather than silently reading as quiet.

Tue Jul 21 evening: US East fell to 293 aircraft (14-day median ~745, i.e. 39% of normal) with all three other regions normal — a clean weekday comparison. The FAA ran ground stops at nine East Coast airports (BWI, BOS, EWR, TEB, LGA, HPN, PHL, DCA, IAD) plus Toronto, with JFK on roughly a four-hour ground delay; about 2,000 flights cancelled and 5,000 delayed, ~7,000 disrupted, LGA worst hit at 408 cancellations. No evidence of any ADS-B feeder or aggregator outage on the date. The instrument read the event correctly and still did not trip: z=-2.695 against a -3.0 threshold — a near-miss worth remembering when judging the threshold.
Conflict share reached 15.244%, +2.59 sd against a weekday-only baseline — same cause as the gdelt_tone note of 2026-07-21. Recorded without a tremble because this v2 series is still in warm-up.
